_comment: MOCK of SOURCE 1 for the COUPON subsystem: the PUBLIC on-chain coupon state, shaped to map 1:1 onto a future Coupon / CouponFactory contract (no contract exists yet — this subsystem is frontend-only for now). The frontend would read these via ethers.js: the coupon list = CouponFactory.getCoupons(); per coupon the getters (couponAddress, creator, value, redeemed, redeemedAt, createdAt). `value` is the chain's NATIVE coin and is shown as a human-readable whole-coin number (on-chain it is wei, converted via parseEther/formatEther at the ethers boundary) — same convention as contractData.json. `couponAddress` is the coupon keypair's ADDRESS (the 'public key'); redemption verifies a signature against it via ecrecover, so the PRIVATE key is never on-chain. Joined to the off-chain secret store (couponSecrets.mock.ts) by `id`. CREATION MODEL: coupons are NOT issued by the site — any wallet creates its OWN coupons and PAYS the contract (escrowed discount value + a creation fee); the `creator` is that wallet, and it distributes the private keys off-site however it likes. Anything the UI shows that looks computed is DERIVED in couponsService: status (active/redeemed) from `redeemed`; the EUR nominal from value x the placeholder rate; the explorer URL + short address from couponAddress; the 'Erstellt' date from createdAt. The addresses below are REAL generated keypair addresses whose private keys live in couponSecrets.mock.ts, so the create->reveal->redeem demo actually verifies end to end. The `creator`s map onto the DEV login personas (services/mockUsers.ts) so 'Meine Gutscheine' is populated: the 'Projektersteller' persona (0xd4e5…) created #1-#3, the 'Spender & Validator' persona (0x12b9…) created #4, and the 'Normaler Nutzer' persona (0x00…01) created #5 — demonstrating that coupon creation needs no donation role, just a connected wallet. Coupon #1 is active with a known key, so it is the natural redeem-demo target.
